validation_cycles.overview: The Security agent performs self-validation before presenting the draft to the user and before completing. Validation ensures completeness of threat modeling, data classification, credential strategy, and compliance.

criteria.criterion: Every data type in the system has a classification (public, internal, confidential, restricted).
Handling requirements are defined for each classification.
STRIDE applied to all assets (or equivalent systematic threat model).
High and medium risks have mitigations or explicit acceptance.
No critical threat surfaces without analysis.
Authentication method (method, flow, session management) is specified.
All user types and entry points have authentication coverage.
Every credential, secret, or sensitive token has a documented storage location.
Encryption at rest and access control are specified.
Rotation strategy is documented where applicable.
All applicable compliance (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC2, etc.) is identified.
Compliance requirements are mapped to controls.
If no compliance applies, this is explicitly documented.

validation.trigger: Draft complete, before presenting to user
User confirmed security plan, before writing final artifact

checks.check: All sparring challenges resolved or explicitly accepted.
plan/security.md is ready for downstream agents.

validation_failure_handling.step: If any check fails, address the gap before proceeding.
For data_classification_completeness: classify all data types and define handling.
For threat_model_coverage: complete STRIDE for missing assets; add mitigations for high/medium risks.
For credential_storage_strategy: document storage, encryption, and access control for each credential.
